defends: A recoverable point-in-time snapshot of the config tree: the archive is created AND verified (stable sha256, extractable, every in-scope file captured), never assumed.
note: FIXED scope (WORKING_METHOD §1). include_roots '.' = archive the whole target. The SHARED vendored/cache/non-IOPHON dirs (.git, node_modules, plugins, projects, cache, etc.) live in manifests/_exclude.json and are merged in at runtime; this `exclude` array holds only backup-specific exclusions: prior archives (data/backups, *.tar.gz) and secret files (.env*). The merged set is applied IDENTICALLY to the walk that builds the expected-entry set AND to tar, so the two agree on scope — this is the invariant that lets verify pass on a real tree (expected files == archived members). Before the fix the walk counted 122,693 files but tar archived only 8,815 because the walk's matcher didn't prune nested .git/node_modules the way tar does. The sentinel is injected ONLY into the temp staging copy during self-test — never into the real target. A live --apply run proves capture by asserting every walked in-scope file is present in the archive listing.
